commit.message:
Add PR pre-check gate to patrol: skip chain when no PRs need review

Adds Tools/check-reviewable-prs.sh — a fast shell script that checks
for open, non-draft PRs with green CI and no unresolved change requests.
Exits 1 if nothing to review, 0 if reviewable PRs exist.

Wired as a deterministic gate step (step 0) in the PR Review Patrol
template. When no PRs are ready, the gate fails and the chain stops
immediately — zero premium LLM tokens spent.

Filters: draft, skip-agent-review label, peel:* labels (already reviewed),
CI not passing, CHANGES_REQUESTED review decision.

commit.message:
Fix MLX embedding crash: null buffer guard, retry, memory throttling (#536)

The app was crashing with EXC_BAD_ACCESS in mlx::core::allocator::Buffer::raw_ptr()
when Metal GPU memory pressure caused buffer deallocation between eval() and
asArray(). This was uncatchable as a Swift error (it's a signal, not an exception).

Fixes:
- Check mlx_array_data_uint8() for null before calling asArray() — converts
  uncatchable EXC_BAD_ACCESS into a thrown MLXEmbeddingError
- Add retry-with-cache-clear in embed(): on buffer failure, clear GPU cache,
  pause 500ms, and retry once
- Track consecutive buffer failures; after 3, abort embedding for the session
  (prevents infinite crash loops)
- Check memory pressure before starting each embedding batch; clear cache if high
- Throttle crown startup enrichment: check memory between repos, add 2s delay,
  skip remaining repos if memory pressure is high

Closes #536

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix MLX embedding crash: prefer overlay pull on low-RAM machines, safer extraction (#536)

Root cause: Crown startup enrichment ran MLX embedding (nomic-embed-text) on
all indexed repos, exhausting Metal GPU memory on 18GB machines. The underlying
MLX buffer was reclaimed by the OS under memory pressure, causing EXC_BAD_ACCESS
in Buffer::raw_ptr() — an uncatchable signal, not a Swift error.

Three-pronged fix:

1. Prefer overlay pull over local enrichment on < 24GB RAM:
   When connected peers have overlay data, request pulls instead of running
   local MLX embedding during startup enrichment. This is the primary fix —
   machines with limited RAM shouldn't be generating embeddings when a more
   powerful peer already has them.

2. Safer GPU→CPU extraction in embedIsolated():
   - Extract full batch as single flat array (1 asArray call instead of N
     per-slice calls), reducing the window for buffer invalidation
   - Do NOT call clearCache() between eval() and asArray() — it can free
     the very buffer being read
   - Add memory pressure check before embedding (clears old cache)
   - Track consecutive buffer failures; abort after 3 (prevents crash loops)

3. Retry with cache clear in embed():
   On buffer-related failures, clear GPU cache, wait 500ms, retry once.
   If retry also fails, propagate the error (don't crash).

Also: throttle crown startup enrichment (2s delay between repos, skip
remaining repos if memory pressure is high).

Closes #536

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Enrich heartbeat with per-repo RAG metadata (#531)

enriched counts, embedding model, and last-indexed timestamp in each
heartbeat. Peers can now see exactly which repos have overlay data
available, enabling data-driven sync decisions.

- currentWorkerStatus() queries local RAG store for per-repo stats
- RAGArtifactSyncDelegate.repoRAGStatuses() protocol method + impl
- swarm.diagnostics includes per-repo RAG metadata in peer info

Closes #531

the filter and the delete call.

commit.message:
Fix overlay auto-pull: allow any role and trigger on peer connect

Two issues prevented overlay auto-pull from working:

1. The ragOverlayAvailable broadcast handler was gated on role == .worker,
   blocking hybrid machines from auto-pulling overlay data from peers.
   Changed to check analyzedCount/enrichedCount > 0 instead.

2. No overlay pull was triggered on peer connect. Added
   initiateOverlaySyncOnConnect() which checks auto-sync mode, finds
   locally indexed repos that the new peer also has, and pulls overlays
   sequentially. Waits 5s for connection to stabilize before starting.

commit.message:
Fix overlay auto-pull: allow hybrid/brain roles to auto-pull from peers

The ragOverlayAvailable broadcast handler was gated on role == .worker,
which prevented hybrid machines from auto-pulling overlay data (analysis
+ enrichment) from connected peers. Since hybrid is the default role,
this meant the crown's laptop never auto-pulled overlays from workers
like Bender even though the RAG tab showed pull buttons.

Changed the guard to check analyzedCount/enrichedCount > 0 instead of
role, so any peer with a local index auto-pulls when a connected peer
broadcasts available overlay data.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

improve job restarts for experiments (#316)

* fix(restart): preserve experiment metadata on job restart, remove execute_experiment_run

- restart_job_execution now passes experiment_id, experiment_version, and
  compiler_runtime_context from the existing execution to execute(), so the
  new attempt row retains the same experiment linkage and context

- execute() gains optional experiment_id, experiment_version,
  compiler_runtime_context params (stored on the JobExecution row) and an
  optional exec_spec param that skips fable compilation when the caller
  already has a pre-compiled ExecutionSpecification

- execute_experiment_run() removed as redundant; scheduler_thread.py updated
  to call execute() directly, fetching the JobDefinition and supplying the
  pre-compiled exec_spec to bypass recompilation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(scheduling): defer compilation to execute, remove rerun2runnable

- experiment2runnable no longer compiles the JobDefinition or applies
  dynamic expressions. Instead it evaluates dynamic_expr and stores the
  result as compiler_runtime_context, then returns the raw JobDefinition
  in RunnableExperiment.definition. This eliminates a redundant compile
  pass since execute() always recompiles.

- execute() now owns the deep_union step: when compiler_runtime_context
  is non-empty it merges the evaluated dynamic expressions into the
  compiled spec before submitting to cascade. deep_union is moved to
  execution.py (re-exported from job_utils for test compatibility).

- RunnableExperiment.exec_spec replaced by .definition (JobDefinition).
  scheduler_thread no longer fetches the JobDefinition separately;
  it passes runnable.definition directly to execute().

- rerun2runnable deleted: its logic is fully covered by restart_job_execution
  preserving experiment metadata and execute() applying compiler_runtime_context.

Fix replace_obs_filters applying wrong filters due to observer list ordering mismatch (#38)

`replace_obs_filters` (and the `local_ensemble_da` override block)
incorrectly paired rendered observers with observation names via
`zip(observers, obs_names)`. Since `obs_names` comes from
`template_dict['observations']` and `observers` from the rendered YAML,
order is not guaranteed—and membership can differ when chronicle logic
deactivates observations. This caused filters to be deleted from the
wrong observers.

## Changes

- **`replace_obs_filters` block**: Replace `zip(observers, obs_names)`
with direct iteration over `observers`, reading each name from
`observer['obs space']['name']` in the rendered output. Remove
now-unused `obs_names` variable.
- **`local_ensemble_da` override block**: Apply the same fix to the
name-dependent override loop.

```python
# Before — obs_name sourced from template_dict, order not guaranteed to match observers
for observer, obs_name in zip(observers, obs_names):
    if obs_name not in obs_to_replace:
        continue
    ...

# After — obs_name sourced from the rendered observer itself
for observer in observers:
    obs_name = observer['obs space']['name']
    if obs_name not in obs_to_replace:
        continue
    ...
```

fix: flatten @Nested tests to avoid empty Surefire XML files

JUnit 5 @Nested classes cause Maven Surefire to emit an empty outer
XML file (tests=0) alongside the nested-class XML files. The Testream
junit-reporter's junit-to-ctrf converter crashes when it encounters a
<testsuite tests="0"> element with no <testcase> children.

Refactor all three test classes to use flat methods instead of @Nested.
Each @DisplayName now prefixes the group name (e.g. 'formatPrice - ...'),
preserving readability while generating a single, well-formed XML file
per class that the reporter can process without errors.

feat: hybrid per-user question dedup for board modes

Replace the global used=true-forever approach with a two-layer strategy:

Layer 1 — used=true as a game-lifetime lock:
- Questions are returned to the pool (used=false) when a game finishes
  or is abandoned, not left permanently consumed.
- All pool_question_ids are now returned on game end, not just unanswered
  ones. user_question_history provides the per-user protection going forward.

Layer 2 — user_question_history for 60-day per-user dedup:
- draw_board() now accepts p_user_ids uuid[] and excludes any question
  already in any participant's history. Drawn questions are atomically
  inserted into user_question_history for all participants.
- When a guest joins an existing online/duel game the host already drew,
  the board's question IDs are recorded in the guest's history via
  record_board_question_history() — no redraw needed.
- Duel createGame/joinQueue pre-fetches host's seen IDs and passes them
  as excludeIds to drawForDuel() to avoid host duplicates.
- cleanup_old_question_history() window extended to 60 days (was 30).
  Daily cleanup cron keeps the table bounded, eliminating the need for
  seen_at filters in queries (pure PK point-lookups, sub-millisecond even
  at 100M rows).

New DB functions (migration 20260414000000):
- draw_board(p_exclude_ids, p_user_ids) — extended signature
- record_board_question_history(user_ids, question_ids) — guest join
- reset_board_history_if_exhausted(user_id) — auto-reset at 80% pool seen
- cleanup_old_question_history() — updated to 60-day window

Backend:
- question-pool.service.ts: drawBoard(excludeNews, allowFallback, userIds)
  + recordBoardHistory() helper
- supabase.service.ts: getSeenQuestionIds() removes inline time filter
- game.types.ts: CreateGameDto gets optional playerIds[]
- game.service.ts: passes playerIds to drawBoard; finishSession returns all
- online-game.service.ts: passes hostId on draw; records guest history on
  join; returns all pool questions on finish/abandon
- duel.service.ts: excludes host seen history on draw; records guest history
  on join; returns all pool questions on abandon

Pool sizing impact: a pool of 5k-10k questions comfortably supports
thousands of active users with no repeats within 60 days.

fix: correct 2-player card hover background color

The primary mode card hover state used #34d399 (teal/emerald), a
completely different hue from the primary #4dff00 (lime green).
On touch devices, the hover state sticks after a tap, making the
background appear to permanently change.

Changed hover background to a subtle darkened lime using color-mix,
consistent with a natural hover effect.
